title: Delissea bishopii
intro: Delissea bishopii, also known as Bishop's Mitre, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae, native to Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Delissea bishopii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to make topical applications and poultices.
taxonomy: Delissea bishopii is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Hawaii, specifically found on the Big Island and possibly other islands.
